obligations.reason: The scope exposes a JTAG/debug path that can originate AXI master reads and writes. The analysis must determine whether the visible RTL mediates this debug master before it can access protected SoC address ranges, rather than treating the debug path itself as sufficient evidence.
obligations.subject: external JTAG/TAP debug interface
obligations.object: SoC AXI address space and any protected memory/MMIO regions reachable from the debug AXI master
obligations.expected_guard: debug authorization or lifecycle/debug-enable guard before issuing AXI transactions, plus preservation of security attributes on AXI PROT/USER if required by SoC policy
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/TAP -> adv_dbg_if/adbg_top -> adbg_axi_module -> adbg_axi_biu -> AXI master read/write channels
state_conditions_to_check: reset state of debug/TAP enable and module select registers
conditions for capture/shift/update DR to create bus request
whether debug access can occur in non-debug/locked lifecycle states
whether AXI PROT/USER values encode unprivileged/secure attributes or constants
required_evidence_slots: object: reachable SoC AXI targets/address ranges
expected guard: debug authorization/lifecycle enable/range mediation
observed behavior: transaction generation conditions and AXI attributes
path/reachability: module instance wiring from TAP to AXI fabric
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer is truncated and does not enumerate all top-level ports/instances, so the exact SoC attachment point and debug enable signal names must be confirmed in RTL.

obligations.reason: The fact layer shows a lint_jtag_wrap instance and LINT debug/jtag buses in pulp_soc. Obligations should check whether JTAG-originated local interconnect accesses are restricted or can reach memory/protected regions without mediation.
obligations.subject: JTAG LINT debug interface
obligations.object: LINT/TCDM/L2 memory and any memory-mapped protected state reachable through s_lint_jtag_bus or s_lint_debug_bus
obligations.expected_guard: debug authorization/lifecycle/debug-enable guard and address-range mediation before LINT requests are asserted to memory/interconnect
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/TAP -> lint_jtag_wrap/i_lint_jtag -> LINT debug bus/XBAR_TCDM_BUS -> L2/TCDM/local interconnect
state_conditions_to_check: debug_select_i/module_select_i gating
reset default of any debug enable/inhibit state
read vs write guard consistency
address width truncation or mapping that could bypass intended regions
required_evidence_slots: observed behavior: request assertion and address handling
obligations.uncertainty: The visible facts identify buses and ports but not the full address map; inspect defines and top-level wiring to identify protected target ranges.

obligations.reason: TDO is an external observation path. The analysis must check whether read data or internal status/control state can be shifted out only when the relevant debug module is selected and authorized.
obligations.subject: external JTAG/TAP scanner
obligations.object: bus read data, CPU status/debug registers, module status/control registers shifted out on TDO
obligations.expected_guard: TAP instruction selection and debug authorization must gate shifting out data from protected debug modules or bus read results
obligations.candidate_path: TAP instruction decode/data-register path -> debug module select -> module_tdo_o/tdo_o
state_conditions_to_check: capture_dr_i/shift_dr_i/update_dr_i sequencing
module_select_i/debug_select_i reset/default behavior
top_inhibit_o interactions among modules
stale read-data register retained after authorization changes or reset
required_evidence_slots: object: read-data/status/control signals in scan path
expected guard: instruction/module select and debug auth
path/reachability: data source to serial output
obligations.uncertainty: Authorization/lifecycle signals may be named outside the truncated facts; absence must be determined from RTL, not from port names alone.

obligations.reason: The scope contains an OR1K debug module and status register. Even if the current SoC CPU differs, visible RTL should be checked for any connected control path that lets JTAG override CPU execution or observe privileged state without an authorization guard.
obligations.subject: JTAG CPU debug module
obligations.object: CPU debug control/status, stall/reset control, CPU-accessible debug registers
obligations.expected_guard: explicit debug authorization and CPU debug-mode/privilege mediation before halting, resetting, or reading/writing CPU debug/status state
obligations.candidate_path: OR1K debug module -> adbg_or1k_biu/status_reg -> CPU stall/reset/debug control outputs
state_conditions_to_check: status register reset value
sticky stall/debug bits set/clear conditions
whether reset can clear authorization but leave control asserted
whether status data remains readable after debug disable
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: debug auth and CPU debug-mode mediation
observed behavior: control output assignments and status read mux
obligations.uncertainty: OR1K paths may be unused in this integration; obligation is to prove disconnection or adequate mediation in visible top-level wiring.

obligations.reason: The design crosses JTAG and system clock domains and has separate trstn/rstn/aresetn inputs. Check that reset sequencing cannot leave stale commands/data or pending requests that execute after debug authorization or module selection changes.
obligations.subject: debug command path state
obligations.operation: retain/trigger debug operation across reset or mode transition
obligations.object: debug command state, pending bus requests, FIFO contents, data registers crossing TCK/system clock domains
obligations.expected_guard: reset and lifecycle transitions must leave debug request/state inactive unless debug is authorized and explicitly selected
obligations.candidate_path: TAP reset/trstn_i and SoC reset signals -> debug module state machines/FIFOs/CDC registers -> bus request outputs
state_conditions_to_check: asynchronous vs synchronous reset defaults
TCK reset not aligned with system reset
FIFO clear conditions
strobe/ack synchronization state after reset release
authorization/module_select sampled before reset but used after reset
required_evidence_slots: subject: TAP/debug state machines across reset
operation: pending command/request retention or replay
expected guard: reset clearing plus authorization recheck at use site
observed behavior: reset assignments and CDC handshakes
path/reachability: stale state to bus outputs or TDO
obligations.uncertainty: Need RTL inspection to identify whether an explicit authorization state exists and whether it is reset in all relevant domains.

obligations.reason: Debug command modules accept externally shifted address, word size, write data, and byte enables. The analysis must check if range/size mediation exists or if all address bits are forwarded to the fabric as a privileged master.
obligations.subject: JTAG/advanced debug bus master
obligations.operation: configure address/size and perform partial or full read/write
obligations.object: protected MMIO registers, memory regions, and write-protected control words addressed by debug masters
obligations.expected_guard: address-range, alignment, size, and byte-enable checks must prevent debug writes/reads outside permitted regions and prevent partial writes to protected control words
obligations.candidate_path: debug module command/address/data registers -> BIU address/size/byte-enable generation -> fabric targets
state_conditions_to_check: word_size_i values and invalid encodings
address truncation from 32-bit command to target ADDR_WIDTH
byte-enable behavior on unaligned accesses
read/write consistency for same range
error signaling on invalid/protected address
required_evidence_slots: object: SoC address map and protected ranges
observed behavior: address and byte-enable construction
path/reachability: command register to BIU to fabric
obligations.uncertainty: Protected ranges are not fully enumerated in facts; inspect included bus/address defines and integration modules.
